tldr: You've got 23 live ads but the copy work is thin — six of your carousels are pushing a broken Shopify variable (`{{product.brand}}`) as body text, and four different ads share the exact 'Magnetic silicone bib' headline. Six creatives are pushing past your 21-day refresh window with duplicated hooks, which is where Andromeda's clustering starts throttling delivery.

benchmark.context: You're refreshing at roughly the category median (~21 days), but at your scale in baby gear the top operators are on a 14-day cycle and volume accounts push closer to 10. The bigger issue isn't cadence — it's that a chunk of what you're shipping is copy-duplicate or missing body text entirely, which compresses the effective concept count Andromeda sees.

ads.headline: Magnetic silicone bib
drivers: Body copy is a broken Shopify template variable — `{{product.brand}}` is rendering literally instead of the brand name, which reads as a bug to any parent scrolling
Headline 'Magnetic silicone bib' is duplicated verbatim across four of your live carousels — Andromeda treats these as one cluster and suppresses the weakest
29 days in market, 8 days past your practical refresh window, with zero body-copy differentiation from three sister ads

ads.headline: Magnetic Silicone Bib
drivers: Same broken `{{product.brand}}` template variable in the body — no story, no benefit, no proof point for the parent reading it
Headline is a near-exact match to three other running ads (only the capitalization changes), so retrieval clusters them together
28 days running to the same collection page as its twin — you're paying to serve two versions of the same ad

ads.headline: Magnetic silicone bib
drivers: Broken Shopify variable in the body — the `{{product.brand}}` placeholder never got swapped out before launch
Generic product-descriptor headline with zero hook — no transformation, no problem-solved, no parent-voice
29 days live, tied for the longest-running creative in your account with no rotation

ads.headline: Magnetic silicone bib
ads.body: "Necessity is the mother of invention" ⚡️ Easy to snap on. Tough for little ones to pull off. Designed by parents who know mealtime.
drivers: The 'Necessity is the mother of invention' body copy is duplicated word-for-word on a second running ad (the one launched two days earlier), so both are competing in the same semantic cluster
Generic 'Magnetic silicone bib' headline pulls no weight — the actual hook (parent-designed, hard for baby to pull off) is buried in the body where fewer people read it
29 days in market on a product-page destination with no refresh, no new angle

ads.headline: "Necessity is the mother of invention" ⚡️ Easy to snap on. Tough for little ones to pull off. Designed by parents who know mealtime."
drivers: Same broken `{{product.brand}}` variable in the body — the whole message is being carried by the headline alone, which is 24 words long and gets truncated in-feed
Headline has a trailing stray quote mark (`mealtime."`) — sloppy detail that reads as low-effort to a parent
The exact same 'Necessity is the mother of invention' copy is running as body text on another live ad, so you're duplicating your best angle across two variants

next_step.urgency: Six ads cross fatigue in under 10 days — the bib carousels running the broken template variable are first.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Babynetic refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Friend, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: We went through every live ad in your public Meta Ad Library and scored each on six fatigue signals; we don't see your CPM, CTR, or ROAS — those come in on the call.
